steps.title: Use the graph intercepts
steps.content: The graph gives two clean intercepts: $(0,35)$ and $(90,0)$. Because the answer choices include standard-form budget equations, fit the form $a x+b y=630$ in Desmos instead of manually finding slope first.

steps.title: Read the coefficients
steps.content: Desmos returns $a=7$ and $b=18$, so the equation is $$7x+18y=630.$$ This also matches the context: 90 T-shirts at 7 dollars each or 35 sweatshirts at 18 dollars each both use the full 630-dollar budget.

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: Choice B is $7x+18y=630$. The other standard-form choice swaps the costs, and the slope-intercept choices have positive slope, which does not match a fixed-budget line. Choice B is correct.
